title: Great Pyramid of Giza Built
description: The Great Pyramid was built as a tomb for Pharaoh Khufu and stood as the world's tallest structure for over 3,800 years.

title: Stonehenge Completed
description: Stonehenge's massive sarsen stone circle was completed around 2500 BC, though construction spanned over 1,500 years.

title: Code of Hammurabi
description: One of the oldest deciphered legal codes, Hammurabi's laws covered trade, wages, theft, and family relations.

title: Trojan War (Traditional Date)
description: The legendary ten-year siege of Troy by Greek forces, immortalized in Homer's Iliad.

title: Traditional Founding of Rome
description: According to legend, Romulus founded Rome on April 21, 753 BC after killing his twin brother Remus in a dispute.

title: First Olympic Games
description: The ancient Olympic Games were held in Olympia, Greece every four years, originally as a religious festival honoring Zeus.

title: Birth of the Buddha
description: Siddhartha Gautama, who became the Buddha, was born in Lumbini (modern-day Nepal) and would found Buddhism.

title: Confucius Born
description: Confucius, the Chinese philosopher whose teachings shaped East Asian culture, was born in 551 BC in the state of Lu.

title: Battle of Marathon
description: Athenian forces defeated the Persian army at Marathon, and a messenger supposedly ran 26 miles to Athens to announce victory.

title: Parthenon Completed
description: Built atop Athens' Acropolis as a temple to Athena, the Parthenon remains one of the greatest architectural achievements.

title: Alexander the Great's Conquests Begin
description: Alexander of Macedon launched his campaign against Persia at age 22, ultimately creating an empire stretching to India.

title: Great Wall of China Construction
description: Emperor Qin Shi Huang unified existing walls into the Great Wall to protect China from northern nomadic invasions.

title: Julius Caesar Assassinated
description: Julius Caesar was stabbed 23 times by Roman senators on the Ides of March, a murder that plunged Rome into civil war.

title: Birth of Jesus
description: Most historians place the birth of Jesus of Nazareth between 6–4 BC, despite the calendar bearing his name.

title: Roman Colosseum Completed
description: The Flavian Amphitheatre (Colosseum) could hold 50,000–80,000 spectators watching gladiatorial contests and public spectacles.

title: Mount Vesuvius Erupts
description: The eruption of Vesuvius buried Pompeii and Herculaneum under ash and pumice, preserving a snapshot of Roman life.

title: Constantine Legalizes Christianity
description: The Edict of Milan granted religious tolerance throughout the Roman Empire, ending persecution of Christians.

title: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
description: Romulus Augustulus, the last Western Roman Emperor, was deposed by Germanic chieftain Odoacer, ending ancient Rome.

title: Birth of Muhammad
description: The Prophet Muhammad, founder of Islam, was born in Mecca around 570 CE. His revelations formed the Quran.

title: Hijra – Islamic Calendar Begins
description: Muhammad's migration from Mecca to Medina in 622 CE marks year 1 of the Islamic calendar and the birth of the Muslim community.

title: Charlemagne Crowned Holy Roman Emperor
description: Pope Leo III crowned Charlemagne emperor on Christmas Day 800, uniting much of Western Europe for the first time since Rome.

title: Viking Raids Begin
description: The raid on Lindisfarne Monastery in 793 CE is considered the start of the Viking Age of expansion and plunder.

title: Battle of Hastings
description: William the Conqueror defeated King Harold II, fundamentally changing English culture, language, and governance.

title: First Crusade Launched
description: Pope Urban II called for Christian knights to recapture Jerusalem, launching two centuries of religious warfare.

title: Magna Carta Signed
description: King John of England sealed the Magna Carta, establishing for the first time that everyone, including the king, is subject to the rule of law.

title: Genghis Khan Unifies Mongols
description: Temujin was proclaimed Genghis Khan in 1206, leading the Mongols to build the largest contiguous empire in history.

title: Marco Polo Reaches China
description: The Venetian explorer Marco Polo arrived at the court of Kublai Khan, spending 17 years in Asia and inspiring European curiosity.

title: Black Death Reaches Europe
description: The bubonic plague killed an estimated 30–60% of Europe's population between 1347–1351, reshaping medieval society.

title: Gutenberg's Printing Press
description: Johannes Gutenberg's movable type press revolutionized the spread of knowledge, enabling the Renaissance and Reformation.

title: Fall of Constantinople
description: Ottoman Sultan Mehmed II conquered Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire and shifting trade routes to the sea.

title: Columbus Reaches the Americas
description: Christopher Columbus landed in the Bahamas on October 12, 1492, opening the door to European colonization of the Americas.

title: Vasco da Gama Reaches India
description: Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama opened a sea route from Europe to India, transforming global trade.

title: Sistine Chapel Ceiling Completed
description: Michelangelo completed his iconic ceiling fresco, including The Creation of Adam, after four years of painstaking work.

title: Luther's 95 Theses
description: Martin Luther nailed his 95 Theses to the church door in Wittenberg, igniting the Protestant Reformation.

title: Magellan's Circumnavigation
description: Ferdinand Magellan led the first expedition to circumnavigate the globe, proving Earth is round.

title: Fall of the Aztec Empire
description: Spanish conquistador Hernán Cortés captured Tenochtitlan, destroying the Aztec Empire and establishing New Spain.

title: Copernicus Publishes Heliocentric Model
description: Nicolaus Copernicus proposed that the Earth orbits the Sun, revolutionizing our understanding of the solar system.

title: Shakespeare Born
description: William Shakespeare, history's greatest playwright and poet, was born in Stratford-upon-Avon, England.

title: Spanish Armada Defeated
description: England's navy, aided by storms, destroyed Spain's 'invincible' fleet, establishing English naval dominance.

title: Galileo's Astronomical Telescope
description: Galileo Galilei turned his improved telescope to the sky in 1609, discovering Jupiter's moons and confirming heliocentrism.

title: Mayflower Pilgrims Land
description: Puritan Pilgrims crossed the Atlantic on the Mayflower, founding Plymouth Colony and signing the Mayflower Compact.

title: Newton's Laws of Motion
description: Isaac Newton published Principia Mathematica, laying out his three laws of motion and the law of universal gravitation.

title: Peter the Great Modernizes Russia
description: Peter the Great founded St. Petersburg in 1703 as Russia's 'window to the West', symbolizing the country's modernization.

title: American Declaration of Independence
description: The thirteen American colonies declared independence from Britain on July 4, 1776, founding the United States.

title: French Revolution Begins
description: The storming of the Bastille on July 14 triggered a revolution that toppled the monarchy and spread democratic ideals across Europe.

title: Watt's Steam Engine Patented
description: James Watt's improved steam engine patent in 1769 powered the Industrial Revolution, transforming manufacturing and transportation.

title: Haitian Revolution
description: Led by Toussaint Louverture, Haiti became the first country founded by a successful slave revolt in 1804.

title: Napoleon Becomes Emperor
description: Napoleon Bonaparte crowned himself Emperor of France in Notre-Dame Cathedral, cementing his transformation of Europe.

title: Battle of Waterloo
description: Napoleon's final defeat at Waterloo ended the Napoleonic Wars and led to his exile on the remote island of St. Helena.

title: Latin American Independence Movements
description: Simón Bolívar and José de San Martín led liberation movements that freed most of Latin America from Spanish rule.

title: Photography Invented
description: Louis Daguerre invented the daguerreotype, the first practical photographic process, capturing the world on silver-coated plates.

title: Darwin Publishes On the Origin of Species
description: Charles Darwin's theory of evolution by natural selection fundamentally changed biology and humanity's view of itself.

title: American Civil War Begins
description: The deadliest conflict in American history was fought between the Union and Confederate states over slavery and states' rights.

title: Suez Canal Opens
description: The 193-km Suez Canal connected the Mediterranean and Red Seas, dramatically shortening sea routes between Europe and Asia.

title: Alexander Graham Bell Patents Telephone
description: Alexander Graham Bell patented the telephone in 1876, making his first call: 'Mr. Watson, come here—I want to see you.'

title: Statue of Liberty Unveiled
description: France's gift to America was dedicated on October 28, 1886, becoming a global symbol of freedom and democracy.

title: Eiffel Tower Completed
description: Built for the World's Fair, Gustave Eiffel's 300-metre iron tower was initially derided but became Paris's beloved icon.

title: X-Rays Discovered
description: Wilhelm Röntgen discovered X-rays and took the first medical X-ray image of his wife's hand, revolutionizing medicine.

title: First Modern Olympic Games
description: The modern Olympics were revived in Athens in 1896, with 14 nations competing in 43 events.

title: Marconi Transmits Radio Signals
description: Guglielmo Marconi transmitted the first transatlantic wireless signal in 1901, ushering in the age of radio communication.

title: Wright Brothers First Flight
description: Orville Wright flew 120 feet in 12 seconds at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina, achieving the first powered heavier-than-air flight.

title: Einstein's Special Theory of Relativity
description: Albert Einstein's annus mirabilis papers revolutionized physics, including E=mc², fundamentally changing our view of space and time.

title: RMS Titanic Sinks
description: On her maiden voyage, the 'unsinkable' RMS Titanic struck an iceberg and sank, killing over 1,500 passengers and crew.

title: World War I Begins
description: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand triggered a chain of alliances, plunging Europe into the first 'World War.'

title: Russian Revolution
description: The Bolshevik Revolution led by Lenin overthrew the tsar and established the world's first communist state.

title: World War I Ends
description: The armistice at 11 a.m. on November 11, 1918 ended the Great War, which killed over 17 million people.

title: Spanish Flu Pandemic
description: The 1918 influenza pandemic infected an estimated 500 million people worldwide and killed 20–50 million.

title: Women's Suffrage in USA
description: The 19th Amendment to the US Constitution granted women the right to vote, a milestone for the suffrage movement.

title: Penicillin Discovered
description: Alexander Fleming noticed that mold (Penicillium) killed bacteria on a petri dish, discovering the world's first antibiotic.

title: Wall Street Crash / Great Depression
description: The stock market crash of 1929 triggered a global economic depression lasting over a decade, with massive unemployment.

title: World War II Begins
description: Nazi Germany's invasion of Poland on September 1, 1939 triggered declarations of war from Britain and France.

title: Attack on Pearl Harbor
description: Japan's surprise attack on the US naval base at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii on December 7, 1941 drew America into World War II.

title: D-Day Normandy Invasion
description: The largest seaborne invasion in history landed 156,000 Allied troops on Normandy beaches, turning the tide of WW2.

title: Atomic Bomb Dropped on Hiroshima
description: On August 6, 1945 the US dropped the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima, instantly killing 70,000 people.

title: United Nations Founded
description: The United Nations was founded on October 24, 1945 with 51 member states to promote peace, security, and cooperation.

title: India Gains Independence
description: India gained independence from British rule on August 15, 1947, after decades of nonviolent resistance led by Mahatma Gandhi.

title: State of Israel Founded
description: The State of Israel declared independence on May 14, 1948, the first Jewish state in 2,000 years.

title: NATO Founded
description: The North Atlantic Treaty Organization was formed in 1949 as a collective security alliance among Western nations.

title: DNA Double Helix Discovered
description: Watson, Crick, Franklin and Wilkins discovered the double-helix structure of DNA, unlocking the secret of life.

title: Mount Everest First Climbed
description: Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay became the first people to reach the summit of Mount Everest on May 29, 1953.

title: Rosa Parks Refuses to Give Up Her Seat
description: Rosa Parks' arrest in Montgomery, Alabama sparked the Montgomery Bus Boycott and galvanized the Civil Rights Movement.

title: Sputnik Launched
description: The Soviet Union launched Sputnik, the world's first artificial satellite, starting the Space Race with the USA.

title: Cuban Revolution
description: Fidel Castro and Che Guevara overthrew the Batista regime, establishing a communist government 90 miles from Florida.

title: Berlin Wall Built
description: East Germany built the Berlin Wall overnight, dividing the city and becoming the Cold War's most powerful symbol.

title: Cuban Missile Crisis
description: A 13-day confrontation between the US and USSR brought the world to the brink of nuclear war over Soviet missiles in Cuba.

title: JFK Assassinated
description: President John F. Kennedy was shot and killed in Dallas, Texas on November 22, 1963, shocking the world.

title: US Civil Rights Act Signed
description: President Lyndon B. Johnson signed the landmark Civil Rights Act, prohibiting discrimination based on race, color, religion or sex.

title: Apollo 11 Moon Landing
description: Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin became the first humans to walk on the Moon on July 20, 1969.

title: Woodstock Music Festival
description: Woodstock drew 400,000 people to a New York farm for three days of peace, love, and music, defining a generation.

title: ARPANET (Internet) First Message
description: The first message was sent over ARPANET on October 29, 1969 — 'lo' (the system crashed before 'login' could complete).

title: Watergate Scandal
description: The Watergate break-in and Nixon's subsequent cover-up led to his resignation in 1974, the only US presidential resignation.

title: Vietnam War Ends
description: The fall of Saigon on April 30, 1975 ended the Vietnam War, with communist North Vietnam reunifying the country.

title: Apple I Personal Computer
description: Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak founded Apple and released the Apple I, helping launch the personal computer revolution.

title: Star Wars Released
description: George Lucas's Star Wars revolutionized Hollywood blockbusters and created a cultural phenomenon that still resonates today.

title: Iranian Revolution
description: The Iranian Revolution replaced the Shah with Ayatollah Khomeini's Islamic Republic, reshaping Middle Eastern politics.

title: AIDS Epidemic Identified
description: The first cases of AIDS were reported in 1981; the epidemic would kill over 36 million people by 2020.

title: Falklands War
description: Britain recaptured the Falkland Islands from Argentina in 1982 in a 74-day war that boosted Thatcher's popularity.

title: Chernobyl Nuclear Disaster
description: The Chernobyl nuclear plant explosion was the world's worst nuclear accident, releasing radiation across Europe.

title: Tiananmen Square Massacre
description: Chinese troops killed hundreds to thousands of pro-democracy protesters in Tiananmen Square on June 4, 1989.

title: Berlin Wall Falls
description: On November 9, 1989, East Germany opened its borders. Jubilant crowds tore down the Berlin Wall, symbolizing the end of the Cold War.

title: World Wide Web Invented
description: Tim Berners-Lee published the World Wide Web in 1991, transforming the Internet into a global information network.

title: Soviet Union Dissolved
description: On December 25, 1991, Mikhail Gorbachev resigned and the USSR dissolved into 15 independent republics, ending the Cold War.

title: Apartheid Ends in South Africa
description: Nelson Mandela became South Africa's first black president in 1994 after decades of apartheid and 27 years in prison.

title: Rwandan Genocide
description: Between April and July 1994, an estimated 500,000–800,000 Tutsi were killed by Hutu extremists in just 100 days.

title: Oklahoma City Bombing
description: The deadliest domestic terrorist attack in US history killed 168 people including 19 children.

title: Dolly the Sheep Cloned
description: Dolly was the first mammal successfully cloned from an adult somatic cell, raising profound ethical questions.

title: Hong Kong Handover to China
description: The UK returned Hong Kong to China on July 1, 1997 after 156 years of colonial rule, under 'one country, two systems.'

title: Google Founded
description: Larry Page and Sergey Brin incorporated Google while PhD students at Stanford, naming it after the number 'googol.'

title: Euro Currency Launched
description: The Euro was launched as an electronic currency in 1999 and as physical coins and notes in 2002, unifying European economies.

title: September 11 Attacks
description: Al-Qaeda terrorists hijacked four planes on September 11, 2001, killing 2,977 people and transforming global security.

title: Human Genome Project Completed
description: The Human Genome Project mapped all 3 billion base pairs of human DNA, opening a new era of medicine and genetics.

title: Facebook Launched
description: Mark Zuckerberg launched 'TheFacebook' from his Harvard dorm room, eventually connecting over 3 billion people worldwide.

title: Indian Ocean Tsunami
description: A magnitude 9.1 earthquake triggered a tsunami that killed over 227,000 people across 14 countries on December 26, 2004.

title: YouTube Launched
description: YouTube was founded in 2005 by three PayPal employees and would transform how humanity consumes video content.

title: iPhone Launched
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the original iPhone on January 9, 2007, revolutionizing mobile computing and launching the smartphone era.

title: Barack Obama Elected President
description: Barack Obama became the 44th US President and the first African American to hold the office.

title: Global Financial Crisis
description: The collapse of Lehman Brothers triggered a global financial meltdown, the worst economic crisis since the Great Depression.

title: Large Hadron Collider Activated
description: CERN's Large Hadron Collider, the world's largest machine, began operation in 2008 and discovered the Higgs boson in 2012.

title: Osama bin Laden Killed
description: US Navy SEALs killed Al-Qaeda leader Osama bin Laden in Pakistan on May 2, 2011, nearly 10 years after 9/11.

title: Arab Spring
description: A wave of pro-democracy uprisings swept the Arab world from 2010–2012, toppling leaders in Tunisia, Egypt, Libya and Yemen.

title: Curiosity Rover Lands on Mars
description: NASA's Curiosity rover landed on Mars on August 6, 2012 and has been exploring the Gale Crater ever since.

title: Higgs Boson Discovered
description: CERN announced the discovery of the Higgs boson — the 'God particle' — confirming the Standard Model of particle physics.

title: Paris Climate Agreement
description: 196 nations signed the Paris Agreement in 2015, committing to limit global warming to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels.

title: Gravitational Waves Detected
description: LIGO detected gravitational waves from two merging black holes, confirming Einstein's 100-year-old prediction.

title: Brexit Vote
description: The UK voted 52% to 48% to leave the European Union in a historic referendum that shocked global markets.

title: COVID-19 Pandemic
description: The COVID-19 pandemic, caused by SARS-CoV-2, killed over 6 million people and transformed life worldwide.

title: US Capitol Stormed
description: On January 6, 2021, supporters of President Trump stormed the US Capitol Building during the certification of electoral votes.

title: James Webb Space Telescope Launched
description: The James Webb Space Telescope launched on Christmas 2021, providing unprecedented views of the early universe.

title: Russia Invades Ukraine
description: Russia launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine on February 24, 2022, triggering Europe's largest conflict since WWII.

title: ChatGPT Launched
description: OpenAI's ChatGPT reached 1 million users in five days and 100 million in two months, sparking a global AI revolution.

title: Aztec Calendar Stone Carved
description: The famous Aztec Sun Stone, often called the Aztec Calendar, was carved during the reign of Emperor Axayacatl.

title: Taj Mahal Completed
description: Emperor Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal as a mausoleum for his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal. It took 22 years to complete.

title: English Civil War
description: England's Civil War between Royalists and Parliamentarians led to the execution of Charles I and Oliver Cromwell's republic.

title: Glorious Revolution
description: William of Orange replaced James II in England's peaceful revolution, establishing constitutional monarchy and parliamentary supremacy.

title: English Bill of Rights
description: England's Bill of Rights established parliamentary sovereignty and constitutional limits on royal power, influencing future democracies.

title: US Constitution Ratified
description: The US Constitution, the world's longest-surviving written national charter, was ratified in 1788 and took effect in 1789.

title: Battle of Trafalgar
description: Admiral Nelson's victory over the Franco-Spanish fleet secured British naval supremacy for a century, though Nelson died in battle.

title: British Abolition of Slave Trade
description: Britain banned the slave trade in 1807, beginning the long process of dismantling the transatlantic slave trade.

title: Industrial Revolution Peaks in Britain
description: By the 1840s Britain was the 'workshop of the world', with railways, factories, and steam power transforming society.

title: California Gold Rush
description: The discovery of gold at Sutter's Mill in 1848 sparked a mass migration of 300,000 people to California.

title: Crimean War
description: The Crimean War saw Florence Nightingale revolutionize nursing, reducing hospital mortality from 40% to 2%.

title: Transcontinental Railroad Completed
description: The golden spike was driven at Promontory Summit, Utah, completing the first US transcontinental railroad.

title: Edison Demonstrates Light Bulb
description: Thomas Edison demonstrated a practical incandescent light bulb in 1879, eventually illuminating cities worldwide.

title: First Gasoline Automobile
description: Karl Benz patented the Benz Patent-Motorwagen in 1886, generally considered the world's first true automobile.

title: Spanish–American War
description: The Spanish–American War ended with the US gaining Guam, Puerto Rico and the Philippines, becoming a global imperial power.

title: Second Boer War Begins
description: Britain's war against Boer settlers in South Africa introduced concentration camps and reshaped British imperial identity.

title: Panama Canal Opens
description: The Panama Canal opened on August 15, 1914, reducing the sea journey from New York to San Francisco by 7,800 miles.

title: Treaty of Versailles
description: The Treaty of Versailles officially ended WWI, imposing harsh reparations on Germany that contributed to WWII.

title: US Prohibition Begins
description: The 18th Amendment banned alcohol in the US from 1920–1933, fueling organized crime and the rise of gangsters like Al Capone.

title: Chinese Communist Revolution
description: Mao Zedong proclaimed the People's Republic of China on October 1, 1949, after defeating Chiang Kai-shek's Nationalists.

title: Korean War
description: The Korean War (1950–1953) ended in armistice, leaving Korea divided at the 38th parallel, still split today.

title: Suez Crisis
description: Egypt nationalized the Suez Canal; British and French intervention backfired, marking the end of European imperial dominance.

title: Yuri Gagarin First Human in Space
description: Soviet cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin became the first human in space on April 12, 1961, completing one orbit of Earth.

title: Vietnam War Escalates
description: US combat troops were deployed to Vietnam in 1965, beginning a decade of devastating warfare that cost 58,000 American lives.

title: Six-Day War
description: Israel defeated Egypt, Jordan and Syria in six days, capturing the West Bank, Gaza Strip, Sinai and Golan Heights.

title: First Email Sent
description: Ray Tomlinson sent the first email in 1971 and chose the @ sign to separate username from host — a convention still used today.

title: OPEC Oil Crisis
description: Arab oil-producing nations embargoed the US, causing oil prices to quadruple and triggering a global economic recession.

title: Microsoft Founded
description: Bill Gates and Paul Allen founded Microsoft in 1975. Their MS-DOS operating system later launched the personal computer era.

title: Camp David Accords
description: President Carter brokered a historic peace agreement between Egypt's Sadat and Israel's Begin, the first Arab-Israeli peace deal.

title: Space Shuttle Challenger Disaster
description: The Space Shuttle Challenger broke apart 73 seconds after launch, killing all 7 crew members and shocking the nation.

title: Nelson Mandela Released
description: Nelson Mandela walked free from Victor Verster Prison on February 11, 1990 after 27 years, setting South Africa on a new path.

title: Gulf War
description: A US-led coalition expelled Iraq from Kuwait in 42 days of combat, but left Saddam Hussein in power.

title: Hubble Space Telescope Launched
description: The Hubble Space Telescope has transformed astronomy, capturing images of the universe's deepest corners for over 30 years.

title: Channel Tunnel Opens
description: The 50-km Channel Tunnel linking England and France via high-speed rail was the engineering achievement of the decade.

title: NATO Expands into Eastern Europe
description: Poland, Hungary and Czech Republic joined NATO in 1999, the first former Warsaw Pact nations to join the Western alliance.

title: Dot-Com Bubble Bursts
description: The dot-com bubble burst in March 2000, wiping out trillions in market value and ending the 1990s internet gold rush.

title: War in Afghanistan Begins
description: The US invaded Afghanistan on October 7, 2001 in response to 9/11, beginning the longest war in American history.

title: US Invasion of Iraq
description: The US-led invasion of Iraq in 2003 toppled Saddam Hussein but sparked an insurgency and regional instability.

title: Twitter Launched
description: Twitter launched in 2006, transforming political discourse and news with its 140-character (later 280) format.

title: Tesla Motors Founded
description: Tesla Motors was incorporated in 2003 by engineers Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning, later joined by Elon Musk.

title: Airbnb Founded
description: Airbnb began when two designers rented out their San Francisco apartment with air mattresses, creating a $75 billion company.

title: Deepwater Horizon Oil Spill
description: The Deepwater Horizon blowout released 4.9 million barrels of oil into the Gulf of Mexico — the largest marine oil spill ever.

title: Fukushima Nuclear Disaster
description: A magnitude 9.1 earthquake and tsunami triggered meltdowns at the Fukushima Daiichi plant, Japan's worst nuclear disaster.

title: First Android Phone Released
description: HTC Dream, the first Android phone, launched in 2008, beginning the rivalry with iPhone that would define mobile computing.

title: First Black Hole Image Captured
description: The Event Horizon Telescope collaboration captured the first image of a black hole at the center of galaxy M87.

title: Greta Thunberg's Climate Strike
description: Greta Thunberg's school strike inspired millions worldwide to march for climate action in the largest climate protest in history.

title: Taliban Retakes Afghanistan
description: The Taliban swept back into power in August 2021 as US troops completed their withdrawal after 20 years of war.

title: Pope Francis Elected
description: Jorge Mario Bergoglio became Pope Francis in 2013, the first Jesuit pope and the first from the Americas.

title: West Africa Ebola Outbreak
description: The 2014–2016 Ebola epidemic in West Africa killed over 11,000 people and triggered a global health emergency.

title: SpaceX Lands Reusable Rocket
description: SpaceX successfully landed and recovered a Falcon 9 orbital rocket booster in December 2015, revolutionizing space launch economics.

title: New Horizons Pluto Flyby
description: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft flew past Pluto in 2015, revealing a surprisingly complex world with mountains and a heart-shaped plain.

title: Donald Trump Elected US President
description: Donald Trump's shock 2016 election victory over Hillary Clinton upended the political establishment and polarized the nation.

title: #MeToo Movement Goes Viral
description: The #MeToo movement exploded in 2017 following Harvey Weinstein exposés, sparking a global reckoning with sexual harassment.

title: George Floyd Murder & BLM Protests
description: George Floyd's murder by a Minneapolis police officer sparked the largest protest movement in US history.

title: Queen Elizabeth II Dies
description: Queen Elizabeth II, Britain's longest-reigning monarch at 70 years, died on September 8, 2022 at Balmoral Castle.
